title: caltha palustris subsp. minor
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. minor, also known as the lesser marsh marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Caltha palustris subsp. minor include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce fever. Caltha palustris subsp. minor can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although its use should be strictly supervised by a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions with medications.
